items: The Attention queue now knows the moment an agent is actually finished. Instead of guessing from when the terminal goes quiet, ReCue listens to each agent's own turn-complete signal, so a finished agent appears in the queue right away — no more short delay, and no more a busy agent briefly flickering in and out.

Agents waiting on you are now labelled precisely: a yellow **Finished** badge when an agent has finished its turn and wants your next instruction, and a red **Needs approval** badge when it's paused waiting for you to approve a tool or permission (Claude and OpenCode; Codex reports finished turns only).

New **Detect finished turns via agent hooks** toggle in Settings → Sessions (on by default). Turning it off opts out entirely — ReCue opens no local listener and simply falls back to detecting finished turns from terminal activity, exactly as before.
